mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01) or shows positive returns in majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the next week during periods of rising inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO declines when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent imposes financing constraints on highly leveraged sectors, increasing default risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 70 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic growth that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or yields negative returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110 index points signals severe manufacturing contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during financial stress periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below 110 during periods of elevated market volatility.
